journey.summary: Travel from Delhi to Mumbai by train/bus. Duration: 4-6 hours. Cost: $40

places.name: Gateway of India
places.description: An iconic arch monument built to commemorate the landing of King-Emperor George V and Queen Mary at Apollo Bunder during their 1911 visit to India.

places.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT)
places.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and an outstanding example of Victorian Gothic Revival architecture in India, serving as a historic railway station.
places.address: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus Area, Fort, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001

places.name: Elephanta Caves
places.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this collection of cave temples primarily dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva features rock-cut architecture dating back to the 5th-8th centuries.
places.address: Gharapuri, Elephanta Island, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400001 (accessible by ferry from Gateway of India)

places.name: Haji Ali Dargah
places.description: A mosque and tomb located on an islet off the coast of Worli, housing the tomb of Pir Haji Ali Shah Bukhari. It's connected to the mainland by a narrow causeway.
places.address: Dargah Road, Haji Ali, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400026

places.name: Bandra Fort (Castella de Aguada)
places.description: A historic Portuguese fort built in 1640 as a watchtower overlooking Mahim Bay, the Arabian Sea, and the southern island of Mahim. Offers panoramic views.
places.address: Byramji Jeejeebhoy Road, Bandstand, Bandra West, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400050

places.name: Afghan Church (Church of St. John the Evangelist)
places.description: A beautiful Gothic Revival church consecrated in 1858, built to commemorate the fallen soldiers of the First Afghan War. Known for its stained glass windows.
places.address: Colaba, Near Navy Nagar, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400005
